title: cordylanthus canescens var. parryi
intro: Cordylanthus canescens var. parryi, also known as Parry's Indian pipe plant, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the figwort family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Parry's Indian pipe plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate coughs, and soothe irritated tissues, making it a valuable remedy for various respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it's essential to consult with a qualified healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
